title: Precious metals retreat from record highs
gist: World Bank Prospects Group data blog: precious metals pulled back in 2026Q2 after 14 months of gains, with gold ~15% below its February peak and silver/platinum ~25% below January highs. Despite the retreat, the Bank projects the precious-metals index up 42% in 2026 then down 8% in 2027, with risks tilted to the upside from geopolitics, policy uncertainty, and market volatility.

insights.claim: Precious metal prices fell in 2026Q2 after 14 consecutive months of gains through February, including a 24% rise in 2026Q1; by June gold was ~15% below its February peak and silver and platinum ~25% below their January record highs.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Marks a tactical correction within a structural uptrend — useful for timing entry into metals exposure.
insights.quote: By June, gold was about 15 percent below its February peak, while silver and platinum were about 25 percent below their January record highs.

insights.claim: The World Bank projects its precious metals price index to rise 42% in 2026 (y/y) before declining 8% in 2027, with risks tilted to the upside.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Sets a base-case price path and an asymmetric (upside-skewed) risk profile for metals allocators.
insights.quote: the World Bank's precious metals price index is projected to rise by 42 percent in 2026 (y/y) before declining by 8 percent in 2027

insights.claim: Gold fell in 2026Q2 as expectations of U.S. rate hikes strengthened and the dollar hit a one-year high in June, plus easing safe-haven demand and profit-taking; even so 2026H1 prices averaged ~37% above 2025.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Confirms rate/dollar sensitivity as the swing factor for gold near-term despite an elevated base.
insights.quote: Gold prices fell in 2026Q2 as expectations of U.S. interest rate hikes strengthened and the U.S. dollar rose to a one-year high in June.

insights.claim: Central bank buying is expected to keep supporting gold while jewelry demand stays subdued at elevated prices; recycled gold (~30% of global supply) and broadly stable mine output mean supply responds only gradually, with gold projected up ~37% in 2026 then down 9% in 2027.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Central-bank demand is the structural floor under gold; a slowdown in those purchases is the key downside trigger to watch.
insights.quote: recycled gold—which accounts for nearly 30 percent of global supply—is likely to remain supported by still-elevated prices

insights.claim: Silver fell ~11% in 2026Q2 after surging 55% in Q1 to all-time nominal highs, yet 2026H1 prices were almost 100% above the 2025 average; it is forecast up 76% in 2026 then down ~7% in 2027.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Silver is the highest-beta precious metal in the forecast, offering the largest 2026 upside but sharper volatility.
insights.quote: silver prices in 2026H1 were almost 100 percent above their 2025 annual average
insights.locator: Silver prices fell by about 11 percent

insights.claim: Silver demand is supported by renewable energy and semiconductors, though substitution in photovoltaic technologies is expected to temper growth in industrial use.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Energy-transition demand underpins silver, but thrifting/substitution in solar caps the bull case.
insights.quote: Silver is a key input in fast-growing sectors, including renewable energy and semiconductors, although substitution in photovoltaic technologies is expected to temper growth in industrial use.
insights.locator: Silver prices fell by about 11 percent

insights.claim: Platinum fell ~27% from its January record by June as mine and recycling supply eased shortage fears, yet 2026H1 prices were ~62% above 2025; the market is still expected to be in deficit in 2026, drawing down above-ground stocks, with prices forecast ~53% higher in 2026 then down ~13% in 2027.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: A persistent supply deficit underpins platinum despite the correction, but palladium substitution in autos is a structural demand risk.
insights.quote: the market is expected to remain in deficit in 2026, drawing down above-ground stocks further
insights.locator: Platinum prices rose to record nominal highs

insights.claim: Risks to the precious-metals forecast are tilted to the upside, with renewed trade tensions, market volatility, or a prolonged Middle East conflict strengthening safe-haven demand, while higher-than-expected rates, easing geopolitics, or slower central-bank gold buying are the main downside risks.
insights.speaker: Jeetendra Khadan, Kaltrina Temaj
insights.so_what: Lays out the explicit risk drivers to monitor for positioning around the base-case metals path.
insights.quote: On balance, risks to the forecast are tilted to the upside.
insights.locator: The price outlook for precious metals remains highly uncertain
